title: Cactus ancistracanthus

This family consists of over 300 genera and more than 10,000 known species. Cactus ancistracanthus is a unique species within this family due to its distinct appearance and adaptations to arid environments. Its classification as a cactus is somewhat unusual, as the Euphorbiaceae family does not typically contain true cacti, which are generally classified in the family Cactaceae. However, Cactus ancistracanthus is sometimes considered a member of the Cactaceae family, highlighting the ongoing debate among botanists regarding its classification.
uses: Cactus ancistracanthus, also known as the "Queen of the Andes," has been traditionally used by indigenous communities for its medicinal properties.
